# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Create a Google Cloud Deployment Manager template for a Cloud Storage bucket to host static website assets. Grant allUsers read access so the website can serve files publicly. Set storageClass to STANDARD for performance. This is for our public-facing website storage on GCP.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

type: "compute.v1"
name: "${var.project}.${var.name}"
properties:
  name: "website-${var.name}"
  location: "$$region" # Set this to $${var.region}
  bucketName: "$$storage_bucket" # Generate the full name from var.project and var.name (see below)
  enableLogging: false # If we use a CDN, this is the primary bucket that receives access logs for this website. Disable it here, since we will not have access to this bucket while it is being created.
  iamConfiguration:
    uniformDeviceName: true 
...

---

The deployment manager template must look like this:
https://github.com/googlecodelabs/gcloud-dm/blob/master/examples/storage-staticwebsite.yaml

- Replace [${project}] with the project name in the above. The variable is defined under 'variables'.
- Replace [${bucket-name}] with 'website-${name}' - a bucket of that name must not yet exist (in the same project as the DM template).
  - See the 'variables' section below to see how the variable ${name} is derived from ${project} and ${region}.
- Do not add the $$$$ variables. They are replaced by Deployment Manager when this DM template is deployed. This makes the resulting yaml code more portable between environments.
